mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding output capacity and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter horizon as investors price in higher future cash flows.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of INDPRO 12-month change vs forward 60-day returns falls below 0.03 or the signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 4% indicates a very tight labor market that compresses margins and triggers Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as monetary policy expectations shift hawkish.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average excess return over baseline is not negative with a t-statistic below -1.65.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 5% and housing starts are declining for three consecutive months, monetary policy is suppressing a key demand sector, leading to recession fears and falling equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward decile spread remains below 2% annualized or the joint condition yields positive returns in more than 45% of occurrences over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, the Fed is forced into a tightening stance that compresses valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as rate hike expectations intensify.
failure_condition: Rank IC of trailing CPI inflation vs forward 20-day returns is lower than 0.03 or the signal fails to produce negative average returns in high-inflation periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending and economic momentum, which boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of RSAFS year-over-year change vs forward 20-day returns is below 0.05 or the series has no predictive power for positive returns in consumer-driven expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below an index level of 100 indicates a contraction in manufacturing activity, which historically precedes economic recessions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is below 100, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than half of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates broad-based expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as the economy operates above potential.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change > 0 and GDPC1 quarterly growth > 0
failure_condition: Joint positive signal produces negative average forward 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances, or the combined Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate over three months signals an active monetary tightening cycle, which immediately increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within a week of the rate trajectory confirmation.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is rising, forward 5-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of events, or the average return is not negative with a 95% confidence interval bound below zero.
